title: Document TUI operator workflow
description: Add a README section describing the new TUI command, supported flags, keyboard controls, filter behavior, refresh behavior, merge confirmation flow, and degraded-mode dependency/install guidance.
acceptance_criteria: README documents `orchestrator tui` usage with `--feature-root` and `--refresh-seconds`.
README lists the supported keyboard controls and explains the three-panel layout at a high level.
README explains that merge is available only for done beads and requires explicit confirmation.
README includes the degraded-mode install hint or dependency note consistent with the runtime implementation.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated `README.md` so `orchestrator tui` now explicitly documents `--feature-root` and `--refresh-seconds`, the three-panel layout, supported keyboard controls, filter anchoring for scoped feature roots, timed versus manual refresh behavior, merge gating to `done` beads with explicit `m` then `Enter` confirmation, and the missing-`textual` retry/install hint (`uv sync` / install project dependencies).
handoff_summary.risks: Documentation was aligned to the current CLI/TUI code paths and spec only. No runtime or test changes were made, so any future TUI behavior changes will require a matching README update.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. This bead only edits `README.md`, so downstream conflicts are limited to other documentation work touching the same TUI section.

conflict_risks: Low. This bead only edits `README.md`, so downstream conflicts are limited to other documentation work touching the same TUI section.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise documentation changes and remaining gaps.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
